name: Stuffing with Sage, Sausages and Roasted Garl
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 pounds Mild Italian sausage, cut into pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
2. To roast garlic: Remove the upper quarter of an unpeeled clove of garlic, moisten the cloves with olive oil, sprinkle thyme and sage over the garlic, and place it in the center of the oven. Roast for 30-35 minutes until tender.
3. In a large bowl, combine bread crumbs and cooled rice.
4. Melt 5 tablespoons butter in a large frying pan over medium heat. Add carrots and sauté until tender (about 2 minutes), then add onion and celery and sauté for an additional 10 minutes. Add green pepper and cook for 1 minute more.
5. Combine the vegetables with the bread and rice mixture in the bowl.
6. In the same pan, sauté sausage pieces over medium heat, stirring frequently until brown and thoroughly cooked (about 8 minutes). Remove sausage pieces with a slotted spoon.
7. Add sausages, chicken stock concentrate, dried sage, salt, and pepper to the stuffing mixture. Pour in chicken stock one cup at a time, mixing well after each addition. The stuffing should be very moist.
8. Press out the garlic flesh from the roasted cloves and add it to the stuffing. Season with additional salt and pepper if desired.
9. Stuff the turkey cavity with the savory mixture.
